title: Tamatiekos – just like my mother's
excerpt: In our house we eat tamatiekos for the sheer pleasure of it, not just when it's cold. There's something comforting about tamatiekos. It's as if you can forgive a lot once you've had a good plate of tamatiekos – even the in-laws. The recipe is enough for 6 people to eat generously. …

seo.title: Tamatiekos - just like my mother's - Marinda Kook

html:
In our house we eat tamatiekos for the sheer pleasure of it, not just when it's cold.  There's something comforting about tamatiekos. It's as if you can forgive a lot once you've had a good plate of tamatiekos – even the in-laws.

The recipe is enough for 6 people  to eat  generously. Maybe there'll be a little left over for tomorrow.

image.alt: Tamatiekos - so van my ma af

items: 2kg beef – I like forequarter, brisket is what I used. You can use lamb too – it needs a bit of fat.
2 onions finely chopped
15 ml oil to fry the onions in
1 sachet or tin tomato paste
5 ml ground ginger (leave it out if you don't have it)

steps: Fry your onions until glassy in a pot on the stove.
Cut your meat into smaller pieces and fry along with the onions until lightly browned.
In a bowl, mix all the remaining ingredients except the potatoes, and pour it over the meat in the pot. Let it simmer gently for 45 minutes.
Peel your potatoes and cut into quarters, and after your pot has simmered for 45 minutes you can add the potatoes to the pot.

Now cook until the potatoes and meat are nice and soft – about 30 minutes. (you'll need to taste as you go). Enjoy your tamatiekos with snow-white rice and vegetables of your choice. You'll feel wonderfully content afterwards.
